title: BIG Drone
body: what use could matt possibly have with a custom-built heavy-lift drone that can carry his body weight over short distances.....?

title: Sub $5k Biped V1 - First Prod Run
description: I took this concept to 90% done with just a couple sub-assemblies left to design + some redesign of weak points, before tariffs and robotics hype took the cost of the project well beyond my grant money and personal financial capability haha. Hope to resurrect this project one day!
body: I took this concept to 90% done with just a couple sub-assemblies left to design + some redesign of weak points, before tariffs and robotics hype took the cost of the project well beyond my grant money and personal financial capability haha. Hope to resurrect this project one day!

title: Sub $5K Biped V0 - Proof of Concept
body:
I designed this as a proof of concept and proof of BOM to show that it was possible to build sub $5k bipedal platforms.

This was before tariffs took hold and the world got crazy about robotics driving up actuator costs etc.

title: General Physical Intelligence Robotic Platform
description: Cheap, fully capable general purpose robot for VLM and RL-based training and research.
body: I designed, built and ROS2-integrated this robotic platform over a 52 hour period (without sleep). It has 2x 7dof Arms and a linear arm stage with 1000N of force output. It is currently being used to finetune a locally-run PI0 model to allow for a fully autonomous, video base reasoning to action pipeline. Click 'Learn more' to see the finished bot and demo video.

title: Reactive Ion Etching Chamber
description: Did planning and research phase for a low-cost Reactive Ion Etching Chamber for Hackerfab@Waterloo. Finished 1st iteration in CAD before tabling it for future.
body: Did planning and research phase for a low-cost Reactive Ion Etching Chamber for Hackerfab@Waterloo. Finished 1st iteration in CAD before tabling it for future.

title: Serial-Parallelized Bipedal Research Platform
description: Working on cost-effective, well designed hardware for the open-source community.
body: Currently designing a Serial Parallelized Bipedal Research platform, aimed at providing a high-quality open-source, biomimetic biped to the robotics community. V1 design is based around a Disney Imagineering research paper exploring the Serial Parallelized Bipedal mechanism for applications in biomimetic movement. Major advantage to this design is a distributed vertical load between two parallel chains, resulting in hip-pitch actuator and knee-pitch actuator acting in one chain, collaborating to generate higher torque in both dimensions.

title: Rotary CNC Mill
description: An automated Lathe designed to incorporate lego-technics electrical components with largely 3d-printed mechanical components.
body: Lead mechanical design for a small team during my 1A system design course. Despite being limited to largely lego and 3d-printed components/hardware, we managed to develop a fully functional CNC Lathe, which could take any given profile and radially engrave it into EVA foam without any user input.

title: 3D Myoelectric Prosthetics
body: I have been designing and building transradial prosthetics for almost 5 years. I began in grade 9 with clunky elbow-actuated designs, before moving to myoelectrics and a much sleeker design in my upper years of high school. Most recently my work includes a study concerning the usage of ultrasound-sourced medical imagery processed through a CNN for high DOF manipulation, and V3 of my 11-dof prosthetic design — the 17th design iteration and 5th fully-redesigned and printed iteration since my first prosthetic.

title: Patch: 3D Printed Transdermal Microneedle Patches
description: Taking a novel approach to AMR bacterial infection treatment through the use of self-administrating medicinal drug patches.
body: As the technical lead of a team of student researchers, I worked on combating Anti-Microbial Resistant Bacterial Infections through Transdermal Patches and micro-needle arrays. I created six different designs for the microneedle cores and arrays, incorporated microfluidic principles for fluid dispensing through microchannels, and created a unique dispensing system that combined with a rate-controlling polymer membrane to release low-viscosity liquid at a consistent rate for adjustable time periods.

title: Foodle: Decentralized Solution to Urban Food Insecurity
description: Blockchain-based social app for food sharing and redistribution in urban communities.
body: After competing in a 24hr youth hackathon with the Foodle concept, I spun it out into a standalone project. I launched the MVP through a Discord community, recruiting members from two different Vancouver communities through door-to-door networking, eventually growing to 32 members. By the end of the 3-month experiment, we distributed a total of $500 worth of produce and $140 worth of canned/containerized food.

title: Mini-Whoop FPV Drone
body: One of my first multi-component assemblies from when I was starting out with CAD in Fusion 360 at age 15.
